/zə.piːn/ Suffix ( pharmacology ) Used to form names of tricyclic compounds used as antidepressants/neuroleptics, antiulcers, anticonvulsants, or for hyperthermia. From azepine .
/zɪlə/ Suffix (humorous, informal, augmentative) Like Godzilla; monstrous, destructive, massive, powerful, wild. Rebracketing of Godzilla .
Suffix ( zoology ) Used to categorise animals based on their evolutionary origin, life history, growth-form or ecological preferences.

Suffix Relating to some specified form of animal existence Relating to some specified geologic period Usage Note Some words (e.g. benzoic ) are derived from -oic . Borrowed from Ancient Greek ζωϊκός (zōïkós), from ζῷον (zōîon, “animal”).
Suffix ( cytology ) Forms names of infectious spores and their lifestages Extracted from sporozoite .
/zə.læk/ Suffix ( pharmacology ) Used to form names of anti-inflammatory agents. From (pyra)zol(e) +‎ -ac (“acetic acid”).

Suffix ( zoology ) Forming nouns indicating individual members of taxonomic groups whose names end in -zoa . ( zoology ) Forming nouns indicating organisms have a particular specified way of life. From Latin -zoon , from Ancient Greek ζῷον (zōîon, “animal”).
/zjəˌmæb/, /zjʊ.mab/, /zəˌmæb/ Suffix ( pharmacology ) Used to form names of humanized monoclonal antibodies. From -zu- (alteration of humanized ) +‎ -mab (“monoclonal antibody”).

Suffix ( biology ) Having or originating from a specified state as a result of conditions in the zygote stage of development. ( genetics ) Having a specified state of allele pairs in the nucleus. ( anatomy ) Having a specified state of physical pairing of structures. From zygo- +‎ -ous .

Suffix (Australia, New Zealand, UK) Forms nicknames, especially of personal names. Usage Note Typically applied to words where the stressed vowel is followed by an /ɹ/. The root is shortened to the stressed syllable, with the suffix replacing the rhotic consonant and truncating the remainder of the word. Originally Australian. From a mutation (specifically a zetacism) of the underlying rhotic consonant /ɹ/ to /z/ (see -z ), combined with -a , a non-rhotic pronunciation spelling of -er (t...
Suffix Archaic spelling of -ae .
Suffix ( rare or nonstandard ) Rare spelling of -ing .
Noun ( firearms ) A firearm chambered for a.22-caliber rimfire cartridge.
/ˈθɹiː fɪftiː ˈsɛvən/ Noun .357 Magnum (a class of firearm cartridge). A firearm chambered for.357 Magnum cartridges. Shortening of .357 Magnum .
Noun ( firearms ) A standard kind of firearm designed to fire bullets that are approximately 0.38 inch in diameter.
Noun ( firearms ) A standard kind of firearm designed to fire bullets that are approximately 0.45 inch in diameter.
Noun ( sports ) An equal number of wins and losses.
Noun ( computing ) An extension for a data file. From the first three letters of "data" ( dat a).

Proper Noun (computing, Microsoft) A set of Microsoft products and services – primarily the.NET Framework, which permits the development of software for a virtual machine in any programming language that is CLI-compliant (i.e., conforms with the Common Language Infrastructure). Usually said to be from network . Called in early press announcements Next Generation Windows Services before this name was chosen. According to Justin Grant, who was part of the team on the Microsoft campus, the name...

Noun ( computing ) An overlay file. From the extension for an overlay file. A file used in memory limited systems to overwrite portions of program execution space (code and data) to enable other operations; such as found in 8-/10-/12-/16-bit systems. Abbreviation of overlay .

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement was meant affectionately. From / + abbreviation of affectionate , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement was meant to be directed at someone. From / + abbreviation of at you , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).
Noun ( 4chan slang ) Synonym of /b/tard . Blend of /b/ +‎ bro .

Noun (Internet slang, 4chan slang) A habitual poster on the /b/ - Random board on 4chan; a member of the /b/ community. Synonym: /b/ro From /b/ - Random , an imageboard on 4chan. It combines a humorously censored version of bastard with /b/ , while rhyming with retard . /b/ itself derives its name from the Futaba Channel (2chan) equivalent.

Noun (Internet slang, 4chan slang) A habitual poster on the /d/ - Hentai/Alternative board on 4chan; a member of the /d/ community. From /d/ - Hentai/Alternative , an imageboard on 4chan, inspired by /b/tard . Taken from degenerate with humorous emphasis on the initial letter in reference to /d/ 's stigma of degeneracy due to its content often veering into sexual fetishism.

Proper Noun ( computing ) A device file, present in Unix and Unix-like operating systems such as Linux, that discards all data written to it and yields no output, always being as an empty file when read. (Internet slang, figuratively) Nowhere; often suggests an intent to ignore specific correspondence. From Unix operating systems, where /dev (the Unix directory containing device files) contains a null entry representing no device.
Symbol Synonym of /rant (“denotes the end of a rant”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is said in a genuine manner. Synonym: /g (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding question is genuine, as opposed to rhetorical. From / + abbreviation of genuine , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”). Coined by Twitter user Katerina Aston (@galactikaa) in 2020.

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding question is genuine. Synonym: /gen From / + abbreviation of genuine question , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is said half-jokingly. Denotes a nonliteral statement with an underlying element of truth or sincerity. Denotes a literal statement that is superficially humorous. Used to introduce uncertainty or deniability of whether a statement is literal or joking: Just kidding, unless…? From / + abbreviation of half-joking , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is an inside joke. From / + abbreviation of inside joke , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

/ˌslæʃ ˈdʒeɪ/ Particle (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is said in a joking manner. Adjective ( Internet slang ) Joking. Noun ( Internet slang ) A joke. From / + abbreviation of joke or joking , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is said in a lighthearted (not serious) manner. From / + abbreviation of lighthearted , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Pronoun (computing, humorous) I (the person writing) Usage Note Unlike the pronoun I , this is used with the third-person form of the verb. From IRC (Internet Relay Chat) and related systems, in which this command generates a text-based action performed by a user: for example, /me dances around might produce the message John dances around .

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is not directed at the participants of the discussion or the followers of the author. From / + abbreviation of nobody here , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement has a negative connotation. Synonyms: /nc, /ng From / + abbreviation of negative (compare neg ), using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement has a neutral connotation. From / + abbreviation of neutral , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is not meant to be forced on someone. From / + abbreviation of not forced , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the writer is not mad or otherwise upset. From / + abbreviation of not mad , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is said in a non-serious manner. From / + abbreviation of non-serious or not serious , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is of nonsexual intent. From / + abbreviation of nonsexual , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Noun (Internet slang, 4chan slang) Someone (male) aligned with the values and beliefs of one's imageboard or community. According to 4chan researcher Sal Hagen, the term with the slashes was first used on the 4chan /pol/ board by an anonymous user who posted on August 7, 2015: "TRUMP IS /ourguy/". The term reached its peak popularity on 4chan in early 2017 before rapidly declining.

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement was meant platonically. From / + abbreviation of platonic , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Noun (Internet slang, 4chan slang) A habitual poster on the /pol/ - Politically Incorrect board on 4chan; a member of the /pol/ community. Synonym: /pol/tard From /pol/ - Politically Incorrect , an imageboard on 4chan; and the word Polack, a slur used against Polish people that depicts them as backwards.

Noun (Internet slang, 4chan slang) A habitual poster on the /pol/ - Politically Incorrect board on 4chan; a member of the /pol/ community. Synonym: /pol/lack From /pol/ - Politically Incorrect , an imageboard on 4chan. Taken from political to indicate /pol/'s main theme. Portmanteau with retard.

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement has a positive connotation. From / + abbreviation of positive (compare pos ), using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement was meant romantically. From / + abbreviation of romantic , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Noun ( Internet slang ) A habitual poster on the /r9k/ - ROBOT9001 board on 4chan; a member of the /r9k/ community. From Robot9000, an open-source chat moderation script created by the American cartoonist and author Randall Munroe, used by the board to temporarily mute members who post content identical to earlier content, used in order to discourage spam and low-effort posting.

Symbol (Internet slang, humorous) Denotes the end of a rant. Synonym: /end rant The use of a slash is derived from HTML code, where the character is used to indicate the end of an element (for example, marks the end of bold formatting). See also [Term?] .

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding question is rhetorical. From / + abbreviation of rhetorical , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol ( Internet slang ) Indicates a lack of seriousness or a resumption to a circlejerk. From / + abbreviation of rejerk , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

/ˈslæʃˈɛs/ Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is sarcastic. Based on HTML code, in which a slash indicates the end of an element (for example, marks the end of bold formatting). Originally written as (to mark the end of a sarcastic message) with the block of text enclosed (see ), this was later simplified to /sarcasm and eventually just /s .
Symbol Denotes the location of a signature on the original, signed copy on file. Abbreviation of signature .

Particle (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is said in a serious manner. Adjective ( Internet slang ) Serious. From / + abbreviation of serious , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol (Internet slang, tone indicator) Denotes that the preceding statement is to be interpreted as teasing. From / + abbreviation of tease or teasing , using the tone indicator format of slash-prefixing set by the earlier /s (“sarcasm”).

Symbol ( Internet slang ) Denotes that everything of worth has already been stated in a given message board thread. The use of a slash is derived from HTML code, where the character is used to indicate the end of an element (for example, marks the end of bold formatting). See also [Term?] .
/ˌtiˌtiˌtiˈti/, /ˌtiːˌtiːˌtiːˈtiː/ Proper Noun ( Internet slang ) The 4chan imageboard /lgbt/. Synonym: 4tran Referencing the purported over-representation of trans women among users of the board.

/ə- -ɪŋ/ Usage Note The common practice is to hyphenate this circumfix’s first element and the word it inflects (e.g.: “a - running”); unhyphenated spellings (e.g.: “abreaking”) are rarer, but nonetheless correct. The a is a reduced form of the preposition on , which was used in Old English with nouns indicating actions or states (e.g. iċ wæs on huntoþe , “I was hunting”). definition: Phrase Supposed inscription at the gate of hell. From Dante Alighieri's work Inferno , translated by Henry Francis Cary as “all hope abandon ye who enter here”, from the Italian lasciate ogne speranza, voi ch'intrate . /ˈæb.səˌlut ˈblɑk.ɪŋ/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ˈblɑk.ɪŋ/ Noun ( railway ) A system of managing trains that breaks the line into small segments, and if a train is in a segment, no other train may follow, unless the second train is following the first, with caution.
/ˈæb.səˌlut ˈsi.lɪŋ/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ˈsi.lɪŋ/ Noun ( aviation ) The maximum altitude above sea level that an aircraft or missile can maintain horizontal flight under normal conditions.

Noun ( Internet slang ) Cinema that is personally considered to be outstanding. (Internet slang, figurative) Any event or work personally considered to be outstanding or amusing. From an image macro of American filmmaker (born 1942) Martin Scorsese captioned with the phrase. In 2022 and 2023, the image and phrase began to circulate around Twitter as an Internet meme.
Noun ( computing ) Code which uses only absolute addresses.
Noun ( grammar ) The use of a comparative to indicate a relatively high degree of some quality, without explicitly making a comparison, as in " higher education ".

Noun ( set theory ) The set that contains exactly those elements belonging to the universal set but not to a given set. Usage Note In mathematical expressions, the absolute complement of a given set A is denoted by "A c ."

/ˈæb.səˌlut ˈkɑn.stn̩t/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ˈkɑn.stn̩t/ Noun ( mathematics ) A constant that maintains the same value wherever it occurs; such as π. Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see absolute,‎ constant.

Noun ( statistics ) The absolute value of the difference between a given value (such as mean or expected value) and a variate value (usually an observed value). ( target shooting ) The shortest distance between the center of the target and the point where the projectile hit or burst.
Noun (mathematical analysis, dated or historical) Ricci calculus; the rules of index notation and manipulation for tensors and tensor fields, as developed by Gregorio Ricci-Curbastro.
Noun (UK, climatology) A period of drought where there has been no precipitation for at least 15 days.

/ˈæb.səˌlut ˈi.ɡoʊ/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ˈi.ɡoʊ/ Noun ( philosophy ) The Fichtean ego that posits its own existence and through the opposition of subject and object thus created dialectically evolves the universe.
/ˈæb.səˌlut ɜnˈdɔɹs.mn̩t/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ɜnˈdɔɹs.mn̩t/ Noun An endorsement that is binding only if and when the original endorsee fails to meet the terms.

Noun ( mathematics ) The difference (sometimes defined to be the absolute difference) between the measured or inferred value of a quantity and its true value. ( military gunnery ) Shortest distance between the center of impact or the center of burst of a group of shots, and the point of impact or burst of a single shot within the group. ( military gunnery ) Error of a sight consisting of its error in relation to a master service sight with which it is tested, including the known error of...
/ˈæb.səˌlut fɔɹm/, /ˌæb.səˈljut fɔɹm/ Noun ( philosophy ) The Platonic form of the supreme idea in which all other ideas participate. The subject object relation.
Noun ( statistics ) the number of data points satisfying something

Noun ( geometry ) The single (up to logical equivalence) geometry whose axiomatic system is equivalent to that of Euclidean geometry without the parallel postulate or any alternative. ( geometry ) Any geometry whose axiomatic system extends that of absolute geometry (in the singular sense) and neither assumes nor contradicts the parallel postulate. From 1832; introduced by Hungarian mathematician János Bolyai (1802—1860).
Noun ( astrophysics ) A boundary in spacetime, defined with respect to the external universe, inside which events cannot affect an external observer.

/ˈæb.səˌlut hjuˈmɪd.ɪ.ti/, /ˌæb.səˈljut hjuˈmɪd.ɪ.ti/ Noun ( meteorology ) In a system of moist air, the ratio of the mass of water vapor present to the volume occupied by the mixture, that is, the density of the water vapor component. Absolute humidity is usually expressed in grams of water vapor per cubic meter or, in engineering practice, in grains per cubic foot. Because this measure of atmospheric humidity is not conservative with respect to adiabatic expansion or compression, it is not com...
/ˈæb.səˌlut aɪˈdɪ.ə/, /ˌæb.səˈljut aɪˈdɪ.ə/ Noun ( philosophy ) In Hegelianism, the spiritual principle of which the reality is the expression.

/aɪˈdi.əˌlɪz.m̩/, /aɪˈdɪ.əˌlɪz.m̩/, /ˈæb.səˌlut/, /ˌæb.səˈljut/ Noun ( philosophy ) A philosophy originally developed by certain German idealists such as Hegel and Schelling, which affirms that reality is grounded in cognition as a single, fundamental and unlimited principle of being that unites subject and object.

Noun ( law ) A doctrine providing immunity from suit by private parties for certain government officials and witnesses in judicial proceedings, even if the act or testimony was illegal, false, or made in bad faith.
/ˈæb.səˌlut ɪmˈpɜd.ə.mn̩t/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ɪmˈpɜd.ə.mn̩t/ Noun ( law ) A diriment impediment that prevents a person from marrying, but does not require punishment or an annulment.
Noun ( computing ) An assembly language instruction that uses only absolute addresses.
Noun An instrument whose calibration can be determined by means of physical measurements on the instrument.
Noun ( computing ) A loader that places absolute code into main memory beginning with the initial address assigned by the assembler. No address manipulation is performed.

/ˈmæɡ.nɪ.tjud/, /ˈmæɡ.nɪ.tud/, /ˈæb.səˌlut/, /ˌæb.səˈljut/ Noun ( astronomy ) The intrinsic luminosity that a celestial body would have if viewed from a distance of 10 parsecs or about 32.6 light years.
/ˈæb.səˌlut məˈd͡ʒɑɹ.ɪ.ti/, /ˌæb.səˈljut məˈd͡ʒɑɹ.ɪ.ti/ Noun A true numerical majority, more than 50% of the items being counted.
Noun ( mathematics ) The mean of the absolute magnitude of a function that has both positive and negative values.
Noun The ruler of an absolute monarchy.
Noun A state over which a sole monarch has absolute and unlimited power. ( uncountable ) The rule of such a monarch, as a form of government.
/ˈæb.səˌlut ˈmju.sɪk/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ˈmju.sɪk/ Noun Instrumental music having an intellectual and affective content, which depends solely on its rhythmic, melodic, and contrapuntal structure.
/ˈæb.səˌlut əv ɑn.fluˈɹɑʒ/ Noun A concentration obtained during the enfleurage process, by removal of the alcohol from the extracts of the pomade.
Noun ( computing ) A path or address that identifies a file or web page which includes all the information necessary to find the file or web page with no assumptions based on local context.
/ˈæb.səˌlut pɚˈsə.nl̩ iˈkweɪ.ʒn̩/, /ˌæb.səˈljut pɚˈsə.nl̩ iˈkweɪ.ʒn̩/ Noun The difference between an observed value and a standard value assumed as true.
Noun ( chess ) A pin in which moving away the pinned piece would illegally expose the king to check.

/ˈæb.səˌlut pɪt͡ʃ/, /ˌæb.səˈljut pɪt͡ʃ/ Noun (music, countable or uncountable) The exact pitch of a note as established by its rate of vibration based on a standard scale. (music, uncountable) Perfect pitch; the ability to identify or produce any tone without need of an external reference.
/ˈæb.səˌlut ˈpɹɜ.ʃɚ/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ˈpɹɜ.ʃɚ/ Noun (physics, meteorology) Total pressure in a fluid equivalent to the sum of the gage and the atmospheric pressures.
Noun A form of primogeniture in which sex is irrelevant for inheritance; the oldest surviving child without regard to sex becomes the heir. Synonyms: equal primogeniture, lineal primogeniture

/ˈæb.səˌlut ˈpɹɪv.l̩.ɪd͡ʒ/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ˈpɹɪv.l̩.ɪd͡ʒ/ Noun ( law ) A privilege given to lawmakers to prevent them from being sued for libel or slander due to statements made on the floor, regardless of whether they were made in good faith.

/ˈæb.səˌlut ɹiˈæl.ə.ti/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ɹiˈæl.ə.ti/ Noun ( philosophy ) Ultimate reality, as is unaffected by the beliefs or limitations of any finite being. ( philosophy ) Reality in relation to the divine mind.
/ˈæb.səˌlut ɹaɪt/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ɹaɪt/ Noun An unqualified right; a right to act or refuse to act that is at the sole discretion of the person possessing the right.
/ˈæb.səˌlut skeɪl/, /ˌæb.səˈljut skeɪl/ Noun ( metrology ) A temperature scale whose zero corresponds to absolute zero such as the Kelvin scale or Rankine scale.
Noun ( chess ) A skewer in which the king is placed in check, forcing it to be moved. Compare absolute pin .

/ˈæb.səˌlut speɪs/, /ˌæb.səˈljut speɪs/ Noun ( physics ) The Newtonian concept of an unchanging and unchangeable reference system of spatial (and temporal) coordinates that is the same for all observers
Noun ( physics ) The Newtonian concept of an unchanging and unchangeable reference system of time and spatial coordinates that is the same for all observers. Synonyms: absolute time, Newtonian time

/ˈæb.səˌlut ˈsɪs.tm̩/, /ˌæb.səˈljut ˈsɪs.tm̩/ Noun The system of measurements based on force, and independent of acceleration of gravity.
/ˈtɜmp.ə.ɹə.tjɚ/, /ˈtɜmp.ɹə.t͡ʃɚ/, /ˈæb.səˌlut /, /ˌæb.səˈljut/ Noun Thermodynamic temperature; temperature measured on an absolute scale such as the Kelvin scale.
Noun ( grammar ) The grammatical expression of time reference relative to now, the exact moment of speaking.
/ˈæb.səˌlut tɚm/, /ˌæb.səˈljut tɚm/ Noun ( mathematics ) The constant in a polynomial.

/ˈæb.səˌlut taɪm/, /ˌæb.səˈljut taɪm/ Noun ( physics ) The Newtonian concept of an unchanging and unchangeable reference system of time (and spatial) coordinates that is the same for all observers. Synonyms: absolute space-time, Newtonian time

/ˈæb.səˌlut weɪt/, /ˌæb.səˈljut weɪt/ Noun The weight of a specific number of seeds, used to find the average weight of a single seed. Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see absolute,‎ weight.

( grammar ) An uninflected verb form used to indicate another action performed by the subject of the principal verb.

Noun ( grammar ) case used to indicate the patient or experiencer of a verb’s action. The absolutive case is used to mark the subject of an intransitive verb, as well as the object of a transitive verb (inasmuch as they are codified in the English nominative-accusative system). Some languages that employ the absolutive case include Abkhaz, Basque, Chechen, Dyirbal, Hindi, Inuktitut, Hiligaynon, and Yup'ik.

/ˈæbˌstɹækt ˈæl.d͡ʒə.bɹə/, /ˈæbˌstɹækt ˈæl.d͡ʒɪ.bɹə/ Noun ( algebra ) The branch of mathematics concerned with algebraic structures, such as groups, rings, and fields. From 1860, The London, Edinburgh, and Dublin Philosophical Magazine and Journal of Science

/ˈæbˌstɹækt ɑɹt/, /ˈæbˌstɹækt ɑː(ɹ)t/ Noun Art that does not depict objects in the natural world, but instead uses color and form in a non-representational way.

Verb ( transitive ) To omit the consideration of details regarding (something); to dismiss the details of (something) so as to concern oneself with higher features and more complex/interesting elements.
Noun ( object-oriented programming ) A class that cannot be instantiated and may have abstract method.

/ˈæbˌstɹækt ɪkˈspɹɜʃ.n̩ˌɪz.m̩/ Noun An American genre of modern art that used improvised techniques, such as sinuous linearity, to generate highly abstract forms while allowing the artist spontaneity. [First attested in the mid 20 th century.]
/ˈæbˌstɹækt ɪkˈspɹɜʃ.n̩ˌɪst/ Noun ( art ) An artist who works within the genre of Abstract Expressionism.
Noun (software, object-oriented programming) A class that has an abstract method to create an object.
Noun (software, object-oriented programming) A creational design pattern that decouples the object creation by providing a group of individual factories by defining an abstract factory class.

Noun (harmonic analysis, representation theory, topology) A branch of harmonic analysis dealing with generalised Fourier transforms that act on functions defined on Hausdorff locally compact topological groups.

Noun ( metaphysics ) An idea separated from a complex object, or from other ideas which naturally accompany it; as the solidity of marble when contemplated apart from its color or figure. Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see abstract,‎ idea.
Noun Words that represent concepts rather than physical things
Noun ( object-oriented programming ) A method of an abstract class that has no implementation in that class and must therefore be implemented by all subtyping classes.
Noun (engineering, logic, mathematics) A model that contains an abstraction or abstractions of the reality.
/æbˈstɹækt ˈmju.zɪk/, /æbˈstɹækt ˈmjuː.zɪk/, /ˈæbˌstɹækt ˈmju.zɪk/, /ˈæbˌstɹækt ˈmjuː.zɪk/ Noun Music created with a focus on structure as opposed to title, text, or program; absolute music.

Noun (mathematics, humorous) Details which are straightforward but so tedious that an author or lecturer would rather skip them (especially those of a category theoretical nature). /ˈæbˌstɹækt naʊn/ Noun ( grammar ) A noun that denotes an idea, emotion, feeling, quality, generalization, representation or other abstract or intangible concept, as opposed to a concrete item, or a physical object. Synonym: nomen abstractum
Noun A number used without application to things, e.g. 6, 8, 10; but when applied to anything, e.g. 6 feet, 10 men, it becomes concrete.
Noun (law, real estate) An epitome of the evidences of ownership; the condensed history of title to a particular parcel of real estate, with encumbrances.
Noun A plane of existence consisting of abstract objects.
Noun (set theory, topology) A type of abstract polytope where the connections between its points correspond to the faces, edges, and vertices of a polyhedron.
Noun (set theory, order theory) A partially ordered set that captures the dyadic properties of a normal polytope without specifying its geometric properties.
Noun A scientific publication which concentrates on publishing abstracts of scientific writings.

Noun A term expressing an abstract idea, such as beauty, whiteness, or roundness, without indicating any object in which it exists. ( taxonomy ) A name for an order, genera, or species of things in which there is a combination of similar qualities.
Noun ( software ) a type in a nominative type system that cannot be instantiated.
